item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short but very sweet! Great taste great execution

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great example for short SCP's

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just necro-posting, don't mind me.

+1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Dr Alice for bringing it to my attention that the original image source is not CC compliant (it was deleted after being found to have been mis-attributed on wiki commons). I've now provided another image to replace it (source here; edited by me).

Also thanks to Uncle Nicolini for editing the article with the updated image!

item.title: Re: Image
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the head-up; I'll look into this (and replace it if the image is no longer supported).

item.title: Image
item.content:encoded: Note: While checking licence informations for the german branch I noticed that on 06/10/19 the image was deleted from wikimedia commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'i need to try something'
… and I had to stop, and sigh with a big smile 'cause it made sense right away.

+1, pass go, collect all the upvotes.

item.title: Re: 200+
item.content:encoded: Probably cause it's a good article. But, hey, what do I know.
I have no idea how this got 200+ upvotes.

item.title: Re: One thing
item.content:encoded:
But that's the joke. You're led to expect this to be about something interesting — and it turns out to be the dumbest joke you can possibly make with a monopoly game. The whole article is just an exercise in chicanery.

You can argue that this makes it shallow and misleading — but actually putting an addendum in there that explores the hook would destroy the whole point. It would be a completely different article if it did explore that angle (and the 'get-out-of-jail free' card joke would feel very much out-of-place; like part of another article that was just slapped on at the end).

item.title: One thing
item.content:encoded: Great article, made me do a double take to understand it so +1ing it all the way, but I would like to see another incident log pertaining to what most people thought when first expanding that incident log and that is an incident log of a gameplay that actually has an effect on say the New York stock market or a business in the real world

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Asking the real question here!
Also as harsh as it sounds, Foundation could use this game to make money by working the real market.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and one HECK of a punchline. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So since the get out of jail free card can get you out of prison-like establishments, does that mean you can buy things with the Monopoly money?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stared at the screen for ages before I finally got it. Bravo, Hippo, Bravo; easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing that makes me reluctant about this is that it more closely resembles a -J article than a mainlister. Still, it was a riot, and while I can safely say I saw the ending coming, it didn't take away from my enjoyment of it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The moment I read the line about the subprime mortgage crisis I started grinning uncontrollable, and it didn't let up until the end. I love it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought about that a lot, but I honestly think if I excluded that bit, a significant portion of readers wouldn't get the joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

There's not enough here. It's somewhat humorous, but ultimately we've just got a board game that makes the game things happen in real life. Could've gone somewhere unique, interesting, creepy, or emotional with it, but just went a single joke, which isn't enough to save it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved this one's simplicity, especially the fact that it was so dumb but worked anyway.

One minor quibble is that the punch-line-explainer is a little too on-the-nose for my taste. Calling out the "Get Out Of Jail Free" card at the end takes away some of the "I see what you did there" satisfaction that makes the article otherwise succeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh god dammit you got me

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Translated into german.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this just because the game caused the subprime crisis of 2007. I LOLed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would want to welcome brevity in SCP articles. However, the base description is excessively brief. I don't find it causing the global market crash to be a good element, since the Foundation would be blatantly disrupting normalcy. It's one thing to throw stuff at an immortal reptilian thing; it's another to knowingly alter the stock market via supernatural means.

Also, I find the incident log to be telling a significantly different story than the base description. Perhaps that is intended to be the twist, but I am - sadly- not feeling such sentiments.

item.title: Re: Welcome to the LOLFoundation
item.content:encoded: Incident 3128-073 is the included log; I actually accidentally mislabeled it as '007.log'. I made the fix — thank you for pointing it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This worked for me as a joke, but I admittedly have a bad habit of skimming SCP articles and missing out on broader context that would have made the punchline more predictable (i.e. the Prodecures), and I have no idea how to play Monopoly.

Upvoted in any case. :p

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Warvis This comes off extraordinarily condescending.

Especially lines like:

The log sucks. The joke sucks. Burn the log. Bury the joke.

Are inappropriate for feedback. If you cannot be civil, skip the crit, and leave your vote. The little snarks, jokes, and asides are unnecessary and reflect poorly on yourself.

Avoid snarky feedback in the future, please.

item.title: Welcome to the LOLFoundation
item.content:encoded:
After reading the intro (which among common procedures foreshadows an apparently critical incident) and description I was on the hook for more about this incident and how its effects translate to reality.
Except I was given a log ("3128/incidents/007.log", hm, not the aforementioned 3128-073?…) from an experiment seemingly conducted by guest researcher Astley.

This wasn't even LOL for me, it was foreseeable ("Take your 'Chance' card." - let me guess….) and unoriginal - I mean, really? The old "get out of jail free" joke dressed in a SCP-labcoat?

At this point I also realised that the introduction is nothing more than a thin wrapper to prep the "joke": There's a vague sensation of being trolled misled when intro and description focus on how the scp affects real estate markets in 2007 (resulting in tests suspeneded…Incident 3128-073?) and then reading a "3 D-class play monopoly"-sketch dated 2 years later - what about the ban on testing? Does Log 073 precede Log 007?
This doesn't seem to fit somehow and the log appears disconnected, introducing a completely different effect seemingly only for the purpose of the gag but nothing more than what is barely necesssary to deliver the punchline: Just an audiolog. Noone saw details, while D-class where playing with an scp or walking out of the site… All this seems a bit sloppy.

The log sucks. The joke sucks. Burn the log. Bury the joke.
If the log(s) or and extended description would deliver what the current article (until the log) promises, this could be great.

  • Give us Log 3128-073!1!! Just dig up a little something, maybe how over the course of a couple of games a correlation with some crisis-related event becomes apparent - may take you a bit research but at a quick glance at wikipedia's article on the crisis I can see a lot of numbers diagrams that can provide some ideas of what game-aspect is reflected
  • Make the "joke" a feature: The anomalous properties of game parts should be part of the description, and have potential for more than just the jail-card.
  • If you can't let go of the "joke", then just playing it up to the point where some researcher floods the test chamber with anesthetics when he sees it coming would imo keep this out of the lulzone
  • Fix little inconsistencies, so the reader isn't confused about the chronology of events

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my opening line as with most of my openings lines could very reasonably be in/correctly construed with me being an offensive jackass. I get negative critique on work. It sucks, and as a critic it's my responsibility to deliver my opinion with the courtesy I'd ask any other writer to provide me. I failed here and I'll try not to do it again in the future.

I'd be a massive fuckhead saying that any type of article does not belong on the mainlist. That's directly antithetical to what I think. The only issue I have here, is that it didn't work for me personally. I can easily see someone else enjoying this, and they do.

none of the sweats

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I just read it, but I read the previous in this thread first and answered accordingly.

Now Hippo, I've been living all my life in a culture where people take pride of bullying the fuck out of eachother as some kind of virtue necessary to help others to reach maturity. My skin is thick like a bunker. So don't worry about it because I know it was simply self-defense. However I also must clarify that this was never against you personally, and barely as a writer. I also took this opportunity to vent out some of my frustrations over similar articles, and I would like to apologize as well for the whole situation.

Remember that I am technically a minority (if outspoken) part of the community who didn't like this article. Name a writer here without at least one or two downvotes in their articles and I'll give you a metaphorical cookie.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if you saw it below (or if you will see it at all, since it's in another thread within this discussion), but just to be clear about it: I apologize for jumping down your throat like that.

There are certain things I can be really, really sensitive about; I think you just accidentally hit a trip-wire in my brain and it made me want to yell. I disagree with you on certain points regarding the article's issues, but none of those disagreements justify trying to make you feel bad or treating you disrespectfully. My apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really doesn't do anything for me. I mean, it's well written and all, but it's just sort of… off. It lies smack dab in the uncanny valley for me; it's not funny enough to be a -J and doesn't take itself seriously enough to be a mainlist IMO. It's dumb, it knows it's dumb, and it doesn't care. Aside from the numerous headscratchers previously mentioned in the thread (why they let D-Class play this and why they left the GOOJF card in the box if they did, etc.), the article teases a real story with real consequences over the reader's face and insists on focusing on that one specific gag.

Teetering between Novote and Downvote, I'll probably leave it be.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Zyn is right; this article is staying here forever, hopefully. On the other side…

You go sort out for me which of my articles can't be mainlist skips, then get back to me.

Easy. Anything over -10. My argument doesn't go about that. Heck, it doesn't even go specifically against just this article. I admit that I couldn't reject more the spirits in which this article is based upon, but it's my opinion alone. Everytime I read an article, I expect to be surprised, emotionally moved or otherwise. This failed to do so because it disappointed me instead of subverting my expectations. And that happened because I felt it incredibly out-of-place for what I've been reading in the mainlist for years. Once again, goofy execution ≠ goofy concept. And this critique goes not only for this but also for each article that I believe fall into the very same mistakes.

And no, it's not necessary that you make an article with 5-10 thousand words worth of addenda, but we have a say here which is roughly translated into "don't go bald nor wear two wigs".

E: fixed stuff. Can't english when agitated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t know if you’ve gotten Kakroom and FaustoV confused, but your rebuttal here doesn’t make sense.

No, I think I just carried my frustration from one post over to another and started projecting. When I get frustrated, I start putting on my blinders and not seeing much of anything besides whatever it is that's frustrating me.

If Kakroom is reading this, then — my apologies for leaping on you like that. Same to FaustoV; I do not respond well to any configuration of the phrase "This article does not belong on the site". Like, just typing those words out has started to make me angry all over again.

I'm going to leave this discussion page alone for a while and try to just relax.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if your issue is that my silly article means that we now can't have a serious article…

Their issue was “it wasn't enough to carry the article for me,” which is a completely fair and harmless thing to say. I don’t know if you’ve gotten Kakroom and FaustoV confused, but your rebuttal here doesn’t make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
like see here no. This is the thing. It isn't a dumb idea. It's a dumb idea within a potentially engaging idea.

Just about every idea can be an engaging one. There's an epic yarn hidden in that 'Get-Out-Of-Jail-Free' card, just as there's an extraordinary article neatly tucked away inside the concept of a monopoly game with macroeconomic effects.

I'm down with people not liking my dumb article, but if your issue is that my silly article means that we now can't have a serious article about the Foundation containing a magical monopoly board-game — one that gives this idea the thought and respect that it deserves — then I don't know what to say.

Maybe I should hold off on that Mouse Trap SCP-001 draft. The world might not be ready.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hopping in here to drop a "hey guys, let's keep it civil please" post. People are free to dislike this page as they please; that said, it's currently doing quite well, so it will stay on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't want to go through them one by one, but — aside from one — your points are just flat-out silly (these are special containment procedures, not 'describe everything about it' procedures; more thorough test data presumably exists elsewhere for just about every SCP object). The only issue you brought up that I think has merit is the one about allowing D-Class to play a game that has a macroeconomic impact; that's a problem I had myself while writing it.

I'm fine with someone hating the article; I'm fine with someone down-voting it; I'm even fine with someone telling me why they down-voted it. However, I'm not so fine with someone telling me…

Why this can't be a mainlist skip

Like, wow. Fucking seriously?

This right here is the bullshit that makes me not want to be a part of this community. Tell you what: You go sort out for me which of my articles can't be mainlist skips, then get back to me. We'll go ahead and delete those. How's that sound?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is a dumb idea

like see here no. This is the thing. It isn't a dumb idea. It's a dumb idea within a potentially engaging idea. And it's the kind of dumb idea I could tolerate as like a one-off gag, for a grin, but - well, there's the line, I guess. It wasn't enough to carry the article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I guess it'll be better if I futher explain myself. Here's the thing; it's not just a writer's issue but the reception of it. I mean, I've seen stuff like this fail and crumble hard. Yes, there is, indeed, a trending toward accepting and embracing this kind of counterculture articles.

My real problem is this: This is goofy in the wrong way and can't be taken seriously in-universe. And not just that, the article (adressing GG Crono's statement) should not be a -J either; however, I think it's because it's not funny enough.

Why this can't be a mainlist skip: In summary, this article takes a clever concept worthy of exploration and subverts it to pull off a dumb joke. That's it. That means that the article is goofy in its execution instead of its conceptualization. It's not that kind of goofy shit like that unmovable plastic bag that exists basically to troll the Foundation. These kind of articles are incredibly good at elevating the dread of living in a universe that may work in cartoon logic at random. That is why I usually love and enjoythe hell out of those skips. On the other side, this (and similar cases which I can't name because I don't remember any particular case) pulls off a dumb joke that, otherwise, could not be logical within the Foundation. For example:

- Why isn't there any mention of previous experiments taken before?
- Why did the Foundation allow a bunch of D's to play a macroeconomic cousin of Jumanji?
- If they were experimenting on it using D's, why did they let such a thing like the "get free out of jail" card when they seemed to know beforehand that the monopoly worked perfectly being incomplete?
- When you write something in context of an organization that has managed to cage
the illogical nature of its universe with Concrete and Steel, then you need to give them
more credit with this kind of stuff.

TL;DR: Lost potential, goofy execution, inconsistent in-universe.

Why this can't be a -J either: It's simply not funny enough. -J articles are hard to create because you need to be creative. It doesn't even need to be super clever; from silly weed and poop jokes to smart parodies of our community's idiosyncracy (like the head-cannon -J i.e.). This skip is not structured to be a -J. In its core it's just a regular article with a mismatched end glued at its tail. It only works because nobody expects this in a mainlist article, and that's exactly the problem.

TL;DR: Only funny because it's not a -J.

SUMMARY: I wish I could be more lenient with this. I admit that I enjoy the series IV style, but this countercultural rise of short, dumb articles is just too extreme for my taste. Once again, it's not really any author's fault but its reception by the community.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some folk are going to say this should be a -J, I'm sure. I vehemently disagree. If it were a -J, it wouldn't have been able to so effectively blindside the reader with the punchline.

Take my emphatic upvote, you absolute madman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To clarify, I don't know the motivation behind other articles like this, but in my case, it definitely wasn't a reaction to more Addenda-heavy articles — I just had this incredibly dumb idea and I really wanted to push it out as fast as possible. This became part of the joke itself — I had a really dumb idea and I wanted to see if it worked, so I wrote my article about someone having a really dumb idea and wanting to see if it works. xD

I feel like adding more or exploring the concept would destroy what I like so much about it: It's dumb. Like, incredibly dumb. There's no exploration of the concept because that would make it less dumb (the ending would become a 'cute finisher' rather than a dumb punchline to a dumb joke).

That being said, I am 100% sympathetic to anyone who reads this dumb article of mine and wishes it was not so dumb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I'm gonna have to echo this.

I think it's a reaction to the Addenda-heavy articles that have become the norm in Series IV. People are tired of excessive prose, so there's some movement in the opposite direction. My own SCP-3185 was an attempt at a more simple Series II-style SCP.

However, going too far in the other direction isn't a good idea either. This is a great concept, and could really be explored and made interesting, but it feels like the OP traded off a very good premise for an (admittedly hilarious) joke.

There's enough here to stave off a -1, but I'm too disappointed in the wasted potential to +1. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen an increase of potentially good articles being traded off for some rather cheap jokes, by which I am, sadly, not amused. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was ready for a story about the foundation accidentally causing the housing bubble and making people go bankrupt and then you got me god darn it

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, don't worry about it; it happens. xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chance Card: "You have been elected Chairman of the Board"
D-class test subject joins the O5 Council.

Chance Card: "Make general repairs on all your property"
Containment Breach in every Site under SCP property.

Edit: I was trying to make a new post, not a reply to this, oh god, so sorry. Dunno how to fix this. Site won't let me delete messages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the first SCP to make me cry laughing. 'Nuff said.

+1 mon ami

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, yes, it's clever, but I can't say that it does anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How do you think the real estate market crash happened?

(ha ha but no seriously it doesn't mimic reality that closely; gameplay just manipulates the real estate market)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If someone flipped the table the game was played on out of frustration would that cause an XK class end of the world scenario? Given that the events in reality mimic events in game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or a strain of mono spliced with polio.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If only it could involve monopoles somehow…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn it. You son of a bitch. You got me. +1 for short and sweet with a splash of spice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the machine they used for Smapti broke down, so they just rebuilt it and named it Hippo. How else do you push out so many quality skips so fast?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This actually tricked me. I thought it would just be about the mortgage crisis!

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god, this is great. If it were not for the interest of get out of jail free, and the amazing writing would have downvoted

+1 for making a great execution

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and nice. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how the reader is set up to think that larger stakes like the mortgage crisis will be the key area of this article's interest, but nope! Couldn't have funnelled down towards a smaller story, and the entry is all the better for it. Well done! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops; you're right — pardon. Fixed!
